history.text: How do I make carbonara for 3 people?

To make pasta carbonara for three people, start by cooking 450g of spaghetti or rigatoni until al dente, then drain it reserving a cup of starchy cooking water. In a separate bowl, whisk together two large egg yolks, one whole egg, 50g of finely grated Pecorino Romano (or Parmigiano-Reggiano), and plenty of freshly cracked black pepper until smooth. Meanwhile, fry 100g of guanciale or pancetta in a pan over medium heat until the fat is rendered and the meat is crisp, removing the meat and leaving the rendered fat in the pan. Heat the pan with a little olive oil or butter (optional) just until steaming but not smoking. Remove the pan from heat, toss the cooked pasta in the hot meat fat, and quickly mix in the egg and cheese mixture while stirring vigorously to create a creamy, emulsified sauce, adding reserved pasta water as needed to reach your desired consistency. Finish by stirring in the reserved crispy guanciale or pancetta and seasoning with additional salt and pepper to taste before serving immediately.
